hub.title: Fantasy MX guides and rules
hub.subtitle: Learn how to play every mode in the app: fantasy with a live draft, exact-score pools and Survivor with lives. Available for Liga MX, Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A and Bundesliga.

nav.ctaText: Create your free account and put what you learned into practice.

fantasy.title: How to play Fantasy on Fantasy MX
fantasy.excerpt: Create your league, run the draft, set your lineup and win your weekly head-to-head matchups.
fantasy.intro: Fantasy mode turns every matchday into a competition between you and your friends. You build a team with real players from the league of your choice — Liga MX, Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A or Bundesliga — and every week you face another team from your league head to head. Whoever scores more points with their starters wins.

sections.p: A fantasy league is a group where each participant manages their own team. Your real players score for you: if your striker scores, you earn points; if your keeper keeps a clean sheet, you do too. What makes Fantasy MX different is that you don't just pile up points in a table: every matchday you play a direct matchup against another participant, just like a real league.
When you create a league you choose its name, the competition it runs on and the number of participants; you become the admin and define the scoring rules. To join a league there are two ways in:

list.d: The admin shares an access code or an invite link so you can enter their private league.
From the Discover tab you can find open leagues by name and join directly.

sections.p: Before the draft you give your team an identity: a name, initials and, if you want, your own crest. That's the team your rivals will see every matchday in the matchups.

Every league starts with a live draft: taking turns against the clock, each participant picks players until their squad is complete. Each player can only belong to one team within the league. We have a guide dedicated to the draft.

sections.h: Your squad: starters, FLEX and bench
sections.p: Your squad is organized by position according to the formation your league defines. Only starters score points in the matchup:

list.d: The fixed spots in your eleven: goalkeeper, defenders, midfielders and forwards, in the numbers your league's formation requires.
A wildcard spot you can fill with players from different positions. It gives you room to start whoever is in form.
Your substitutes. They don't score points, but you can swap them with your starters before each matchday.

sections.note: Check injuries, suspensions and the schedule before the matchday kicks off: bench points don't count toward the score.

sections.p: Every matchday the app schedules home-and-away duels between the teams in your league. The matchup score is the sum of each team's starters' points: whoever scores more wins; if you finish level, it's a draw. The result feeds the league table with wins, draws and losses, points for and against, just like a real football table.

sections.h: Strengthen your team during the season
sections.p: Your squad isn't final. Throughout the season you can add available players nobody has picked and propose trades to adjust your team matchday by matchday. Position, team and stats filters help you find reinforcements.
Matchday after matchday, your results accumulate in your league's standings. Whoever drafts best, sets the best lineups and strengthens their team best finishes on top.

draft.title: How the live draft works
draft.excerpt: Real-time turns, a pick timer and auto-pick so the draft never stalls.
draft.intro: The draft is the moment when players get distributed among all the participants of a fantasy league. On Fantasy MX it's a live event: everyone connected at once, picking in turns, with a timer for every selection and instant updates.

sections.p: The draft is a turn-based selection. Each player in the competition can only belong to one team within your league, so once someone picks them, they're gone for everyone else. That's why every turn counts.

The league admin is the one who activates the draft when the group is ready. At that moment every participant gets a notification to enter the room. Until then, the app shows you whether the draft is pending, active or completed.
The room shows the pick order and whose turn it is at every moment. Each pick has a time limit configured by the league:

list.d: The app alerts you when you're up and the clock starts ticking. Pick before it hits zero.
If your league has it enabled and your time runs out, the app picks for you so the draft never stalls. It also helps when you know you won't be able to connect.

sections.p: The room shows who's online in real time, and the draft board records every selection round by round. That way you know which positions your rivals have covered and which gaps they still have.
Come prepared and never depend on a single name:

list.d: Have several targets per position: someone will very likely take your favorite before your turn.
Remember your lineup needs a goalkeeper, defenders, midfielders, forwards and a FLEX. Don't overload one position.
In the search you can filter by position and team, and compare total points, average and games played.
The last rounds are for substitutes who save you on matchdays with absences.

quiniela.title: How to play the pool
quiniela.excerpt: Predict the exact score of every match and compete with your group matchday after matchday.
quiniela.intro: The pool is the fastest, most social mode on Fantasy MX. Every matchday you predict the exact score of the matches and compete with your friends to see who gets the most right. No draft, no team management: just your football knowledge.

sections.p: A pool is a private group where everyone predicts the results of a specific league and stage — for example, the current Liga MX tournament or the Premier League season. Every correct call earns points and the group table updates as matches are played.

You can create your own pool by choosing the league and stage it will run on, and invite your people with an access code or a shareable link. Groups are private: you only compete with the people you invite.

For each match you enter an exact score: goals for the home side and goals for the away side. You can adjust your prediction as many times as you like until the match kicks off; once the ball rolls, it locks. There's only one prediction per match: if you change it before kickoff, it simply replaces the previous one.
Each group defines how many points every type of correct call is worth, always across three tiers:

list.d: You nailed the final score exactly. It's the best-paid call.
You missed the score but called who would win the match.
You predicted a draw and the match ended level, even if with a different score.

sections.note: Points don't stack: if you hit the exact score, you earn only that tier's points. Check your group's scoring rules in the info tab.

sections.p: As matches are played, your pool's standings update live. You can view each matchday's table or the overall tournament total, and show off your calls to your group.

survivor.title: How Survivor works
survivor.excerpt: Pick one winner per matchday without repeating teams. Manage your lives and be the last one standing.
survivor.intro: Survivor is Fantasy MX's elimination mode. Every matchday you pick one team you think will win. If it wins, you stay alive; if it loses, you lose a life. When you run out of lives, you're out. The last one standing wins.

sections.p: Every matchday you pick a single team from the available matches. Your pick lives or dies with that match's result, so choose wisely.
Each participant starts with the number of lives the pool defines (usually one). Here's what happens to your pick every matchday:

list.d: You survive and advance to the next matchday with your lives intact.
A draw costs no life: you survive, though the team is used up.
You lose a life.
Missing the deadline also costs a life. Don't let the matchday slip by.

sections.note: When you reach zero lives you're eliminated from the Survivor. If the pool hands out more than one life, a stumble won't send you home, but every mistake counts.

sections.p: Once you use a team, it's burned for the rest of the tournament: you can't pick it again in later matchdays. That's the real challenge of Survivor — deciding when to spend the favorites.
You can change your pick as many times as you like while the matchday hasn't started. As soon as the first match of the matchday kicks off, it locks completely: no more picking or changing.

Picking the week's favorite isn't enough. Since you can't repeat, look at the schedule for upcoming matchdays and save certain teams for the tough dates. In My Picks you have your full history of wins, draws and losses to plan what's next.
The Survivor ends when the tournament runs out of matchdays or when a single survivor remains, depending on how the pool is configured. Outlast everyone and the glory is yours.

puntos.title: The fantasy scoring system
puntos.excerpt: How goals, assists and real performances become points, and how they decide your matchups.
puntos.intro: In Fantasy mode, your players score based on what they actually do on the pitch. Those points decide your matchups every matchday. This guide explains how they're calculated and why they can change from league to league.

sections.p: After every match, Fantasy MX takes your players' real stats — goals, assists, minutes, clean sheets, cards and more — and turns them into points for your team. It works the same whether your league runs on Liga MX, the Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A or the Bundesliga.
Positive actions add points, like a goal or an assist, and some negative actions subtract, like a red card. The idea is to reward the players who truly influence the match.

The same action can be worth different amounts depending on the player's position. For example, a goal usually pays more for a defender than for a forward, because it's less common. That balances the value of every position in your lineup.

Each fantasy league's admin configures the scoring rules: which actions score, how many points they're worth per position and under what conditions. Two leagues can reward different things, so check your league's rules before building your team.

Your weekly matchup score is the sum of your starters' points, including the FLEX spot. Your bench players accumulate stats, but their points don't count toward the duel: setting a good lineup matters as much as drafting well.

In every matchup you can see the breakdown of how much each of your players scored, and the player search shows their total points, average and games played. It's the best way to understand what worked and adjust your lineup for the next matchday.

estrategia.title: Draft strategy: how to build a winning team
estrategia.excerpt: Tiers, pick order and common mistakes: prepare your draft like a pro.
estrategia.intro: The draft defines half your season: good picks give you a team that competes on its own, and mistakes chase you matchday after matchday. This guide goes beyond the rules — if you don't know them yet, start with the draft guide — and focuses on how to pick better than your rivals.

sections.h: Before the draft: study your league
sections.p: Every fantasy league defines its formation (how many goalkeepers, defenders, midfielders and forwards start, plus the FLEX and the bench) and its scoring rules. Review both before preparing anything: if your league pays a lot for clean sheets, defenders from organized teams are worth more; if it rewards assists well, look for the players who take corners and free kicks.

Instead of a single list from 1 to 50, group players by position into levels of similar value. When your turn comes, don't ask who's the best available — ask which tier is about to run out. If three top-tier forwards remain but only one midfielder, the midfielder is the right pick even if the forward is more famous.

Start with players who play everything: undisputed starters on teams that create a lot. A slightly less brilliant player who plays 90 minutes every matchday usually scores more by season's end than a star who rotates or gets injured. Consistency wins fantasy leagues.

Players who take penalties, free kicks and corners concentrate the plays that end in goals or assists. Between two similar options, keep the taker: they participate in their team's points even on an off day.

sections.h: Goalkeeper and defenders: not too early
sections.p: The points gap between the best goalkeeper and a good one is usually smaller than between the best forward and an average one. Prioritize attack in the early rounds and cover goal and defense in the middle ones, targeting teams that concede little.

sections.h: The FLEX and the bench: your room to maneuver
sections.p: The last spots on your squad are the ones that save seasons. Think of them like this:

list.d: A good extra player from your strongest position is worth double if they can start in the wildcard spot.
Your substitutes should cover different positions to respond to injuries and suspensions: don't stack three backups for the same spot.
If two of your starters rest on the same matchday, your bench must be able to cover both at once.

sections.p: Most drafts are lost for the same reasons:

list.d: Loading up on players from your favorite club rarely maximizes points. Let performance decide, not the shirt.
Last tournament's points don't carry over. Look at the present: current minutes, role in the team and fitness.
If you let the timer run without a plan, you end up picking in a rush or leaving the decision to auto-pick. Arrive with your list ready.
The draft board tells you what everyone needs. If you're the only one without a goalkeeper and two good ones remain, you can still wait a round.

sections.h: The draft doesn't end on draft day
sections.p: Free agents and trades exist to fix things. Spot the in-form players nobody drafted early, and propose trades where both sides win: leagues are won by managing, not just drafting.

ligas.title: Which league should you pick for fantasy? A guide to all 5
ligas.excerpt: Liga MX, Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A and Bundesliga: what changes when you play fantasy, pools or Survivor in each one.
ligas.intro: On Fantasy MX every mode plays the same in any competition, but each league has its own rhythm: format, number of matchdays, kickoff times and style of play. Choosing well makes your season more fun and your predictions sharper.

sections.h: The rules don't change — the football does
sections.p: Fantasy with draft and matchups, exact-score pools and Survivor work identically across the five leagues. What changes is the context: how many teams there are, how long the season lasts, what time matches kick off, and how predictable results are.

Mexican football plays two tournaments per year (Apertura and Clausura) with 18 teams and a short regular season that leads into the liguilla playoffs. For fantasy that means agile seasons where every matchday matters a lot; for Survivor, less margin for error and tournaments that resolve quickly. Its evening kickoffs are ideal if you live in Mexico.

Twenty teams, 38 matchdays and the deepest squads in the world. There are plenty of draft-worthy players, but also more rotation, so following lineups matters. It's the league with the most information available for decision-making.

Spanish football rewards control of the game. The big clubs tend to dominate their matches, which makes for relatively reliable Survivor picks, and their attackers concentrate a lot of fantasy points. Watch out for rivalry matchdays: that's where favorites fall.

sections.h: Serie A: the art of the tight score
sections.p: Italy keeps its tactical reputation: closed scorelines and defenders as protagonists. For exact-score pools, 1-0 and 1-1 pay off more than in other leagues; for fantasy, goalkeepers and defenders are gold thanks to clean sheets.

With 18 teams and 34 matchdays, German football is among Europe's most attacking: high pressing, fast transitions and lots of goals. Ideal for an attack-heavy fantasy squad, and for pools if you like big scorelines — even if the exact score is harder to nail.
Four questions settle the choice:

list.d: The league you can watch live is the one you'll predict best. European leagues play mornings and midday in Mexican time; Liga MX plays evenings and nights.
Play where you know the squads and the streaks: in fantasy, information is the edge.
The best league is the one your friends follow too. The most fun mode is the one you brag about in the group chat.
Short and intense tournament? Liga MX. A long 38-matchday season to manage? Premier League, LaLiga or Serie A.

sections.p: You can join leagues, pools and Survivors in different competitions at the same time. Many people play fantasy with friends in a European league and the family pool in Liga MX: each group picks its competition when it's created.

consejosQuiniela.title: How to win your pool: tips for predicting scores
consejosQuiniela.excerpt: From the likely scoreline to the calculated risk: real strategy for exact-score pools.
consejosQuiniela.intro: Nailing an exact score takes a lot of knowledge and a bit of luck. Over a full season, the difference between first place and the rest of the table is method: understanding the points, playing the likely scorelines and knowing when to take risks. Here's the method.

sections.p: Every pool configures how much the exact score, the correct winner and the correct draw are worth. If the exact score pays much more, taking risks makes sense; if the gap is small, locking in the winner matchday after matchday builds the win. Check the values in your group's info tab before defining your style.
In professional football most matches end with few goals: 1-0, 2-1, 1-1 and 2-0 repeat far more often than any thrashing. When in doubt, predict low. A spectacular 4-3 is almost never the smart play.

Split the prediction into two decisions: first who wins (or whether it's a draw), then the most typical scoreline for that result. Clear favorite at home? 2-0 or 2-1. Tight match between equals? 1-1 or 1-0. Chaining the two questions is more reliable than imagining the score all at once.

The draw is the result almost nobody predicts, and it happens more than it seems: around one in four matches. If your group rewards the correct draw, spotting two or three likely draws per matchday earns you points your rivals are giving away.

sections.h: Use the data already in the app
sections.p: You don't need to leave Fantasy MX to do your homework:

list.d: The real gap between two teams shows in the table and their goal difference, not in the prestige of the crest.
Teams that score little and concede little point to short scorelines; an attack on a streak justifies adding a goal to your call.
The head-to-head between each team's key players tells you which attack arrives in better form.
A big club that played international competition midweek often rotates on the weekend: be suspicious of the tired favorite.

sections.p: You can edit your predictions until each match kicks off: use that. Fill in the whole matchday in advance so no match is left empty, and review your calls once lineups come out. A match without a prediction is free points for your rivals; pools are also won by not failing through neglect.

sections.h: Predict with your head, not your shirt
sections.p: The most common mistake of all: always predicting a win for your team, or a loss for your archrival. Step away from your fandom for a moment and ask what score you'd pick if you didn't support anyone. Your spot in the group table will thank you.

glosario.title: Fantasy MX glossary: speak the language of the game
glosario.excerpt: Draft, FLEX, pick, lives, exact score… every term in the app explained briefly.
glosario.intro: First time in a fantasy league or a Survivor? Here's the vocabulary you'll see across Fantasy MX, grouped by game mode, so no word catches you offside.

list.d: Live event where a league's participants pick players in turns until their squads are complete.
Each individual selection, both in the draft and in Survivor.
The full set of players on your team: starters and bench.
The players you field on a matchday; only they score points in your matchup.
Your substitutes. They don't score, but you can swap them with starters before each matchday.
Wildcard lineup spot that accepts players from different positions.
The weekly head-to-head duel against another team in your league; whoever's starters score more wins.
A player no team in the league owns, available to add to yours.
An exchange of players between two teams in the same league.
Option that lets the app pick for you if your draft time runs out.
Private leagues are joined with an access code; public ones appear for everyone in the Discover tab.
Getting both teams' goals exactly right. The best-paid call.
Calling which team wins the match without nailing the score.
Predicting a draw when the match ends level, with any scoreline.
The moment a match kicks off and your prediction is frozen.
The tournament or season the pool runs on, for example a Liga MX Apertura.
The table that adds up points from every matchday in the stage.
Your margin for error. You lose one when a pick fails or you don't pick in time; at zero lives you're eliminated.
The single team you choose each matchday hoping it wins.
A team you already used in a previous matchday and can't pick again.
The final survivor once everyone else is eliminated: the Survivor winner.
Each round of matches in the real league; it's also the unit of play in every mode of the app.
Finishing the match without conceding; usually earns extra points for goalkeepers and defenders.
The pass that ends in a teammate's goal.
Games played and points per game; you'll see them when comparing players in the search.
Liga MX's knockout final phase, played after the regular season.

sections.p: The rest of the guides explain each mode in depth using these terms in context: if something still doesn't add up, the answer is one guide away.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about Fantasy MX
faq.excerpt: What everyone asks before starting: accounts, leagues, drafts, locks and devices.
faq.intro: Straight answers to the most common questions about Fantasy MX. If yours isn't here, the guides for each mode go into full detail.

sections.p: Yes, completely. Every mode — fantasy, pools and Survivor — is free, with no subscriptions or in-app payments. The project is supported by discreet advertising on content screens.

sections.h: Do I need an account to use the app?
sections.p: Not to follow results, standings and stats: come in and explore any league you like. An account is needed to play — creating or joining fantasy leagues, pools and Survivors — because your teams and predictions are saved to your profile. Creating one takes seconds with your email or Google.
Liga MX, Premier League, LaLiga, Serie A and Bundesliga. Every fantasy league, pool or Survivor is created on one of these competitions, and you can play in several at once.

sections.h: How many people do I need to play?
sections.p: Pools and Survivor work with very small groups. In fantasy leagues, whoever creates the league sets the number of participants: the more there are, the more contested the draft, but a handful of friends is plenty of competition.

sections.h: What if I can't make it to the draft?
sections.p: Turn on auto-pick: when your time runs out, the app will pick for you and the draft will keep moving. When you're back, you can adjust your team through free agents and trades.

sections.h: Until when can I change predictions and lineups?
sections.p: The general rule across the app is kickoff: pool predictions lock when each match starts, your Survivor pick locks when the matchday's first match begins, and your fantasy lineup must be set before the matchday starts.

sections.h: How do I invite my friends?
sections.p: Every league, pool or Survivor has an access code and an invite link to share. Whoever receives it lands straight in your group, no extra steps.

sections.h: What devices does it work on?
sections.p: Any modern browser, and you can install it on your phone as an app (PWA) without going through any store. Once installed, you'll get notifications for your draft, your leagues and your results.

We collect only what's needed to run your account and your games, we don't sell your data, and you can exercise your rights of access, rectification, cancellation and objection at any time. The full details are in our privacy notice, available from the footer.
